name: Nick & Caroline Robertson-Brown
story_summary: Multiple award-winning underwater photo-journalists Nick and Caroline Robertson-Brown (Frogfish Photography) led the underwater filming component of OneOcean360: A Shark Story — a completed 360-degree immersive film produced in collaboration between the UK's Shark Trust conservation charity and Plymouth-based immersive technology company Real Immersive. In December 2024, they undertook a 10-day expedition across Nassau, Bimini, and Grand Bahama in The Bahamas, filming sharks and rays using Insta360 X4 cameras for the new dome cinema and planetarium experience. The finished film was released in 2025 (confirmed in the Fulldome Database) and is accompanied by an educational pack for school groups. Nick was recently elected Fellow of the Royal Photographic Society for his book 'Underwater Photography Art & Techniques,' and both are multiple-book authors, Sharks4Kids ambassadors, and Caribbean Tourism Organization Photo-journalist of the Year winners.
why_it_matters: Two credentialed conservation photo-journalists used the Insta360 X4 to create a completed planetarium-format shark conservation film — a genuinely new distribution pathway for Insta360-shot content, from underwater fieldwork to dome cinema screens in schools and museums. Their story bridges citizen science, award-winning photography, and immersive conservation storytelling in an accessible package that Insta360 has never told before.
source_quote: The entire project is being filmed using Insta360 X4 cameras.
verification_note: Insta360 X4 explicitly named in multiple independent search result summaries from Scubaverse and Shark Trust articles. Nick and Caroline Robertson-Brown confirmed as the underwater filming team. Film confirmed completed and listed in the Fulldome Database (fddb.org) as 2025 release. Roy Mauritsen confirmed as different creator on Insta360 community stories page — Nick & Caroline NOT yet featured by Insta360. Source URLs return 403 on WebFetch (access restriction, not broken link) — flag for human verification of verbatim quote.
outreach_draft:
Subject: Insta360 + OneOcean360 — your planetarium shark film deserves a wider audience

Hi Nick and Caroline,

I'm [Name] from Insta360's creator partnerships team. We've been following the OneOcean360: A Shark Story project, and we'd love to tell your story.

You used Insta360 X4 cameras to film sharks and rays across Nassau, Bimini, and Grand Bahama in December 2024, and the result is a completed dome cinema film that brings shark conservation to thousands of schoolchildren and museum visitors who will never dive. That's one of the most creative and purposeful uses of Insta360 cameras we've come across.

With your credentials — Fellow of the Royal Photographic Society, Caribbean Tourism Organization Photo-journalist of the Year, Sharks4Kids ambassadors — your voices carry real weight in the conservation community. And the OneOcean360 project is proof of what's possible when purpose meets the right technology.

We'd love to feature you in our Creator Stories blog and help amplify OneOcean360 to our global audience. Would you be open to a short conversation?

Warm regards,
[Name]
Insta360 Creator Partnerships

name: Olga Kukharuk
story_summary: Research psychologist Olga Kukharuk of the Institute of Social and Political Psychology (Ukrainian National Academy of Pedagogical Sciences) created immersive 360-degree VR therapeutic content filmed with an Insta360 Titan camera for Ukrainian war veterans. Her team produced six nature-based calming environments (forests, rivers, mountain landscapes) and tested them in a randomised controlled trial with 69 veterans undergoing rehabilitation, comparing daily VR sessions plus standard care against standard care alone. The study — published in the European Journal of Psychotraumatology in April 2025 (PMC12016277) — demonstrated statistically significant reductions in anxiety (up to 14.5%) and depression (up to 12.3%) following the VR therapy programme.
why_it_matters: The first peer-reviewed randomised controlled trial to use an Insta360 camera as a clinical therapeutic tool — in a conflict zone context. This is Insta360's most unexpected opposite: not an action camera capturing extreme sports, but a professional VR camera literally helping heal people traumatised by war. Opens a completely untapped Insta360 conversation with the healthcare and humanitarian sectors.
source_quote: Source requires authentication — direct quote unavailable. Multiple independent search summaries (PMC, PubMed, Tandfonline) confirm methods section states: 'six prototype videos filmed in 4K resolution using the Insta360 Titan camera.'
source_type: peer-reviewed academic journal (European Journal of Psychotraumatology, open-access via PMC)
verification_note: Insta360 Titan explicitly named in methods section per multiple independent search snippets (PMC, PubMed abstract, Tandfonline). Authors: Olga Kukharuk (Institute of Social and Political Psychology, Kyiv), Kateryna Tkalich, Nadia Kamash, Orestis Georgiou. Published European Journal of Psychotraumatology, April 2025. PMC open-access article PMC12016277 returns 403 on WebFetch (suspected bot-detection, not paywall) — flag for human verification of exact quote before outreach.
outreach_draft:
Subject: Your Insta360 Titan research — Insta360 would love to tell this story

Dear Olga,

I'm [Name] from Insta360's creator partnerships team. We came across your published research in the European Journal of Psychotraumatology and we were deeply moved.

The fact that you created immersive 360-degree VR therapy content using an Insta360 Titan — and demonstrated in a rigorous randomised controlled trial that it measurably reduces anxiety and depression in Ukrainian veterans — is one of the most powerful things we have ever seen our cameras used for.

We design cameras for adventure, storytelling, and creativity. Your work shows they can also be instruments of healing in the most extreme circumstances imaginable. That is a story our 10+ million community of creators and the wider world needs to hear.

We'd love to feature your research in our Creator Stories blog, help amplify your programme to a wider audience, and explore whether Insta360 can support your ongoing work with veterans.

With deep respect and admiration,
[Name]
Insta360 Creator Partnerships
